subject: Project Management
title: The DOD-STD-2167 Standard
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The DOD-STD-2167 Standard.

hook.text: In 1985, the U.S. Department of Defense made waterfall mandatory for all military software projects.

buildup.text: DOD-STD-2167 codified sequential development: requirements first, then design, code, test, deploy. No going back.

discovery.text: The standard spread beyond defense. If the Pentagon required waterfall, corporate America adopted it too.

twist.text: By 1994, the DOD itself acknowledged the standard was flawed and replaced it with MIL-STD-498, allowing iteration.

climax.text: For nearly a decade, the world's largest buyer of software mandated a process its creator said wouldn't work.

punchline.text: When the Pentagon says 'do it this way,' the whole industry follows — even into failure.

quiz.question: What did DOD-STD-2167 do?
options: Made waterfall mandatory for U.S. military software projects
Required agile methods for defense projects
